title: Dipladenia fragrans
intro: Dipladenia fragrans, also known as Mandevilla, is a flowering evergreen vine native to South America, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to alleviate stress and anxiety. The bioactive constituents of Dipladenia fragrans include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Dipladenia fragrans is classified within the family Apocynaceae. This family of plants is commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The genus Dipladenia is closely related to Mandevilla and is often included in the same genus by some botanists. However, in botanical nomenclature, Dipladenia is recognized as a distinct genus. Dipladenia fragrans is a flowering plant native to South America.
